meta_title: For Freelancers: portfolio building to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Portfolio Building to Solve Deploy Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Use this beginner-friendly checklist to build a robust portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to impress clients fast.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures stem from complex environment configurations. The solution is to standardize your stack using Antigravity for automated infrastructure and Claude Code for iterative development. By building a modular portfolio, you bypass manual hosting hurdles, demonstrate technical competency, and deliver production-ready applications that clients can actually interact with immediately.
content.intro:
You’ve spent days crafting the perfect logic, only to watch your terminal vomit a wall of red error text the moment you attempt to ship. The 'it works on my machine' syndrome is the silent killer of freelance careers, turning high-potential developers into perpetual tinkerers who never launch. You aren't just missing a deployment; you are missing the credibility that comes with a live, functional portfolio.

The shift from 'coder' to 'AI Builder' happens when you stop fighting infrastructure and start leveraging it. By integrating Antigravity for automated deployment and Claude Code for intelligent iteration, you transform your workflow from a manual struggle into a repeatable, high-velocity machine. It is time to stop hiding your code and start showcasing your impact.

steps.title: Define Your Infrastructure Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define your environment requirements. Antigravity needs a clear blueprint to handle your dependencies and environment variables automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements: [Insert Tech Stack]. Generate an optimized Antigravity config file that prioritizes zero-config deployment and secure environment variable injection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate antigravity.yaml based on existing package.json'

steps.title: Initialize the Claude Code Agent
steps.description: Establish a deep-context session within your project directory. This allows Claude Code to understand your entire file structure before you start building.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a project documentation structure that explains the architecture for a potential client viewing my portfolio.

steps.title: Build Modular, Deployable Features
steps.description: Focus on building small, isolated modules. This makes debugging easier and allows you to deploy specific features to your portfolio incrementally.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this module into a standalone component that can be deployed independently within the Antigravity ecosystem.
steps.claude_code_command: claude task 'refactor core logic into modular components'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Connect your repository to the deployment provider. Use Antigravity to ensure every push to the main branch triggers a successful build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that validates build success before pushing to the production environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for antigravity pipeline'

steps.title: Verify and Portfolio-Link
steps.description: Perform a final audit of your live environment. Ensure your portfolio reflects the latest, cleanest version of your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my current deployment state and provide a summary of the live infrastructure for my portfolio documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify deployment status and generate README summary'

pro_tips: Always use environment-specific secret masking in your Antigravity config to prevent credential leaks.
Treat your README.md as a sales page; include a 'How to Run' section that highlights your architectural decisions.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your deployment scripts to catch environment-specific bugs before they reach production.
Keep your portfolio projects under 500 lines of core logic to ensure rapid deployment and high readability for clients.
Leverage Antigravity's caching layer to shave minutes off your build times during iterative updates.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, iterate by adding a secondary service to your Antigravity stack and documenting the process to create a 'Case Study' repository for your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting deployment friction hold your career hostage. Master the stack, ship your work, and become the developer clients fight to hire. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Focus on building small, isolated modules. This makes debugging easier and allows you to deploy specific features to your portfolio incrementally.
